directions.name: Keep the restored control flow and add A-side row-pair lookahead inside the 64x64 PTX microkernel
directions.hypothesis: The restored surface is correct again and the headline bottleneck profile still looks like compute underfeed, not DRAM saturation: tensor activity is 38.55%, short scoreboard is 6.80%, mio throttle is only 0.33%, and barrier is low. The right-left-right-left B sweep was negative, so the next human Ps2r branch should move to the A side instead of the B side. The current microkernel still loads one row pair, consumes all columns, then loads the next row pair. Keeping the next A row pair live while the current row pair is consumed is the cleanest warp-local test that matches the human Ps2r idea without touching CTA staging or control flow.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Warp-local shared-to-register latency on the A-fragment path inside the 64x64 PTX microkernel.
directions.risk: Moderate. This is warp-local and correctness-safe in shape, but it can increase live A fragments and push register pressure above the current 167/thread surface.
directions.stop_condition: Reject if register pressure rises materially or if short-scoreboard changes do not produce a measurable hot-band win.

directions.name: Keep the restored surface and test a light L2-friendly logical CTA swizzle
directions.hypothesis: The user's L2-cache idea remains the strongest non-warp-local alternative. With correctness back under control, a light logical CTA swizzle over the hot-band grid is a clean way to probe inter-CTA locality without changing the inner microkernel or shared-memory layout.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Inter-CTA L2 reuse across neighboring hot-band B tiles.
directions.risk: Moderate. The previous grouped traversal regression means this needs to stay lightweight and reversible.
directions.stop_condition: Reject if L2 throughput stays flat or if runtime regresses on the restored surface.

directions.name: Try warp-specialized Pg2s staging on the restored surface without changing the tile shape
directions.hypothesis: The last major human-idea family that remains mostly untested on the restored correct surface is Pg2s orchestration. Even with low barrier, all warps still participate in the hot-band staging loop. A minimal producer/consumer split that leaves the macro tile untouched could reveal whether some tensor issue is still being diluted by CTA-wide staging responsibilities.
directions.expected_bottleneck: CTA-level staging orchestration and barrier dilution inside the hot-band loop.
directions.risk: High. This is a larger orchestration change and can easily hurt tensor issue if the producer/consumer split is wrong.
directions.stop_condition: Reject if barrier falls but tensor activity and hot-band time do not improve, or if the split inflates registers materially.

notes: Round 9/50 resumes forward optimization from a restored correct surface. The recommended next move is dir_01, which follows the human Ps2r idea on the A side while keeping control flow and CTA staging unchanged so the result stays easy to interpret.
